name: Bird, William Edwin
description: Birdie is a preacher's son, In every way a typical one; Recites strange things he has done, Dollars made and ladies won. Innocently he puffs away, Even Bird will burn some day.

name: Black, John Merryman
description: Holy is his name, Odd is his face, Old is his fame, Lost every race, Yearns a horse to "place."

name: Blanck, Walter Lipps
description: We see the barber treat him, As resigned to fate he sits, Lighting up his new cigar, Trying if it fits. Ever when he's playing goal, he's Roaring out "come back" to Fritz.

name: Bloomfield, Arthur Leonard
description: Bloomy thinks he has a voice— Lordy, what a back-fence noise! Oil it, songster, grease your chords— On the stage they're flat as boards. Mum's his motto; hates to talk. Ye gods, what a wobbly walk!

name: Oscar Ellis Bransky
description: O, Bransky of the game of chess is king; So day and (k)night its praises he doth sing. Chess champion to be is his aircastle; A queen to win he'd pawn his cap and tassel. Rendered in England Oscar's name is h— Os(s)car.

name: Distler, Carl Martin
description: Commanding, noble, silent, Always sedate and strong, Retreating—never violent, Legs twenty-three feet long.

name: Doetsch, Ernest Pohl
description: Extra tall and strong—of beard, Prince of jokes and stories weird, Doleful 'til exams he cleared.

name: Edmund Doleweczynski
description: Digs the well of knowledge deep. Open face with prying peep. Loyalest of all the class; Lives but class dues in to pass. Youth to hypnotize a lass.

name: Daniel Ellison
description: Dan has an appetite for work, And in his courses did there lurk Nothing that his brain would shirk.

name: Abiathar William Field
description: Bummed for two years in the class; Interested more in a lass. Loves to talk about the West. Life he thinks is one big jest.

name: Hiram Fried
motto: "Dropped from the frying-pan into the fire."
description: Forked tongues would never work on Hiram's name— Revenge so earthly would seem somewhat tame, If one were sure of getting this by wire Emerging from where Fried's past all desire—

name: Rogers Galt
description: Gee! who is this with frame so spare And face so young? Of course, you ponder. Lest you should doubt, we must declare That this is Galt, our baby wonder.

name: German, John Slade
clubs: Nominating Committee Y. M. C. A., (S.)
description: Billy German's as strong as an ox, In fact, he hails from Woodberry's rocks; Lest, however, you misjudge him, Lest your favors you begrudge him, You must know Bill's baldheaded as any the docs.

name: Griffen, Wallis
clubs: Member of Board of Governors of Cotillion Club, (J.)
description: Worries not the slightest bit; Aye, with smiles his face is lit. Loves a comfortable place; Lives but troubles to efface, In good humor all the day; Sleep when not disturbed holds sway.

name: William Rickert Glen
clubs: Recording Secretary Y. M. C. A., (J.)
description: Bill's bill is quite a projection, Its color is part of the flag. Loud he howls on protection: "Long wave the star-spangled rag."

name: Gow, Alexander Murdoch
motto: "Go to the ant, thou sluggard," Once a wise man said. "Wait," answers Murdoch, "at least until I'm dead."

name: Grauer, Albert
description: Alternately, altruistic and agnostic aggravator; Loving labyrinths linguistic, logic lacking loon, lost later.

name: Harding, Weston O'Brien
description: Just a little bit clumsy and awkward; Inordinately fond of grub. Must have three or four meals per diem; Moreover, you just ought to see 'im. Yet withal he's a pretty good cub.

name: Harris, Henry Patterson
description: Pat at Dago is a shark, Attacked Poor "Useless" in the dark, Ten is now his daily mark.

name: Hendry, Ernest Singleton
description: Elections holds he in his hand, Selects the man who's sure to land. Hey, presto! the magician's wand.

name: Hills, Edward Tremaine
description: Teddy's quiet, don't say much; Ever works "to beat the Dutch." Dimpled, sweet-faced, neat and such. Don't hold your breath until he talks: Years you'll wait, for here he balks.

name: John Mallory Holmes
description: Delighted with a bicker, Uncompromising kicker, Could never be boot-licker, Knows anyone a slicker, Young future fighting vicar?

name: Hunting, Warren Belknap
description: Warranted quite safe and sane, Although the girls will be his bane. Rather shy, which doesn't matter; Rhetoric at times he'll scatter. Endeavor once to cut him out— Nothing to it, put to rout.

name: Hynson, Henry Parr
description: Punctual at class each day, Attendant at Y. M. C. A.; Royally he leads the ball; Runs always for Levering Hall.

name: Iglehart, Iredell Waddell
description: Innate leader in sport athletic; Goes about with exceedingly dignified air. Let us summon to aid the powers prophetic; Eminent politician will be his share.

name: Knipp, Arthur Russell
description: Knowledge, muscle, good in a tussle, Never would have need of a bustle. If old Robin Hood still flourished, Priest he'd have as nicely nourished— Probably we'd have the luck Of seeing a close fight, Knipp and Tuck.

name: Lynch, Edmund Calvert
clubs: Chairman J. H. U. Indoor Games Committee
description: Decided opinions of his own, he knows the whole shebang of it; Over the ocean he did roam, he certainly got the hang of it. Couldn't see Doc for his mustache, didn't he get awfully rash.

name: Macsherry, Richard
description: Dare-devil Dick Is up to every trick; Curly head, curly brain, Knows how to bluff—but, alas, 'tis vain.

name: William Bose Marye
description: Bosey is a philo-so-fer ; Is a very genteel loafer. Loves to run o'er hill and through dale Lagging on to Schwarz's coattail, Yet he truly is Euterpe's daughter.

name: Addis Emmet Mosely
description: Most seen pacing near the second stairway, Or before the door that's known as Kirby Flowers ; Sentinel-like, Doc. Barrett bound to waylay, Em's the great boot-licker of this class of ours.

name: Walter Emil Myers
description: Excitement never reaches Walter; Moves like a wagonload of ice. In music holds forth on a psalter, Live artist, good at any price.

name: Arthur Rankin Padgett
description: Awful killer of one girl; Thinks she is the only pearl. Seen him yet out for a lark, Post at Monument and Park? Ah! she comes; they flit away Down the street—this, every day. Guess what 'tis they have to say.

name: Parelhoff, Bernard Mark
description: Modest? No, but quite bombastic; Acts as if he owns creation. Rather than see his plans go wrong, he Kicks and raves like Carrie Nation.

name: Perce, Le Grand Windfield Jr.
description: Perce's shyness is absurd, Ever afraid to be seen or heard; Rather perish than say a word. Can do anything in sight, Except, of course, to do the right.

name: Pietsch, Charles Frederick
description: Chubby face, Handsomelocks; Of course, he often Loves loud socks. Liked to grind some years ago, Yet now he's part of "Loafer's Row."

name: Pleet, William
description: Piccadilly much in sight, Lovely hair combed out just right, Extra-ultra-stylish clothes, Elite in his every pose, Tell no tales anent his hose.

name: Isaac Potts
description: Prim and proper little man On abbreviated plan; Thinks, no doubt, but says no say, Temper sweet and mildest way, Sober each and every day.

name: George Pitts Raleigh
clubs: Delegate to Board of Governors Athletic Association, (J.), (S.)
description: Hopes to be a man some day, Always kind-er talks that way, Ma thinks he's a kid to stay.

name: Reinhardt, William Andrew August
description: Ready to say his lessons or run, Equally ready for study or fun; Interested in brushing his hair, Never neglecting his work up there; Yet he is a porcupine for fair.

name: Reynolds, Stanley Meade
clubs: Member Board of Governors Cotillion Club, (J.), (S.)
description: Many were his occupations— Editor, manager and debater; An expert in all vocations; Destined to be, sooner or later, Executive of the elevator.

name: Rolker, Herman Frederick
description: Fair as a lily, Rosy and sweet; Innocent, gentle, Tidy and neat. Zounds! what feet.

name: Schmeisser, Harry Christian
motto: "All's safe, Walter, Harry's at the goal."
description: In years to come he's bound to please; a Lecturer is Harry. His tongue doth roll Long words and wise, words such as these, which tell You,

name: Jordan Herbert Stabler
description: Buck the line! Go right through! Upon the field in portly view, Cussing, cheering, brings good luck, Kicking, smirking Manager BUCK.

name: Stewart, George Adolph
description: Gracious, goodness, what a pull! And his head just like a bull. Sarsaparilla, ha!

name: Strouse, David Eli
description: Doughty doer of devilish deeds, Actual only for him who heeds, Venus and Mars his governing gods, Except for the vulcan he has in his hods.

name: Tough, Frederick Bevan
description: From morn to night he loves to talk— Rather tough on those around; Everyone doth say with fervor, "Go Dig and labor 'neath the ground."

name: Ware, Joseph Todhunter
description: Joe with the girls right soft, not hard-WARE is; Join them in marriage his intention is. O, no! he'll be no Mormon—just a preacher; Of voice has more than any mortal creature. Eggo in biology is his feature. Ah! 'We hereby elect, by common consent, Mr. Reggie DePeyster Guelf, "Class beauty", because, as you will assent, We think he's a class in himself. M.
